type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
There are aspects of the Cancarb process that are quite difficult to control. For example, we are unable to
control the ambient (air) temperature on the day of the test or indeed on any other day. Given that the
reactions that can possibly generate CO in the process (especially in the Waste Heat Duct) are complex
and varied, changes in the ambient air temperature and consequently in the duct, can alter the equilibrium
composition of these reacting species (C, CO, CO2, H2O and possibly H2 and CH4). For example, the
oxidation of CO to CO2 is favored by high temperature (endothermic reaction) but the reaction of C and
CO2 to form CO as well as the reaction of C and steam to form CO are favored at lower temperatures. The
year over year variations in CO will therefore most likely continue even if we do exactly the same thing. We
have started to look at these scenarios with the aim of deepening our understanding and possibly coming
up with an operations strategy that can enable us control CO very effectively.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Sulfuric Acid is used to control pH in cooling tower water. Sulfuric acid was used above thresholds but is
fully neutralized and as such there are no releases or disposals to water other than small releases to air
tank venting.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Significant bag house filter issues were experienced in 2015 during our annual stack testing. Significant effort was put in to research more reliable bag house filters, and were completely replaced at a cost of $150,000 to reduce emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: During 2015 we had significant issues with our bag house filter reliability during our annual stack testing. Significant measures were taken to identify better quality filter bags and all filters were replaced at a cost of $150, 000.00 to reduce PM 10 emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Significant issues were experience with our bag house filtration unit in 2015 during the time of our annual stack testing. All filtration bags were replaced with a better quality system at a cost of $150,000 to reduce bag house emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: There are aspects of the Cancarb process that are quite difficult to control. For example, we are unable to control the ambient (air) temperature on the day of the test or indeed on any other day. Given that the reactions that can possibly generate CO in the process (especially in the Waste Heat Duct) are complex and varied, changes in the ambient air temperature and consequently in the duct, can alter the equilibrium composition of these reacting species (C, CO, CO2, H2O and possibly H2 and CH4). For example, the oxidation of CO to CO2 is favored by high temperature (endothermic reaction) but the reaction of C and CO2 to form CO as well as the reaction of C and steam to form CO are favored at lower temperatures. The year over year variations in CO will therefore most likely continue even if we do exactly the same thing. We have started to look at these scenarios with the aim of deepening our understanding and possibly coming up with an operations strategy that can enable us control CO very effectively.

comments.comment: There are aspects of the Cancarb process that are quite difficult to control. For example, we are unable to control the ambient (air) temperature on the day of the test or indeed on any other day. Given that the reactions that can possibly generate CO in the process (especially in the Waste Heat Duct) are complex and varied, changes in the ambient air temperature and consequently in the duct, can alter the equilibrium composition of these reacting species (C, CO, CO2, H2O and possibly H2 and CH4). For example, the oxidation of CO to CO2 is favored by high temperature (endothermic reaction) but the reaction of C and CO2 to form CO as well as the reaction of C and steam to form CO are favored at lower temperatures. The year over year variations in CO will therefore most likely continue even if we do exactly the same thing. We have started to look at these scenarios with the aim of deepening our understanding and possibly coming up with an operations strategy that can enable us control CO very effectively.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Stack : The CO emission rate for 2011 is higher than that for 2010.  A closer look at the raw data used in the analysis indicates that there were a few ‘spikes’ in CO ppm values during the measurements. Unfortunately, these spikes were not treated as outliers but were used in obtaining averages that were subsequently used in the analysis (9.4% of the data used accounted for 51% of the average). The result is that these average values are higher than would have been obtained if the spikes were treated as outliers, leading to higher CO emission rate estimates. Such major outliers were not observed for 2010.  From operations perspective, four units were in operation during the 2010 tests while five units were in operation during the 2011 tests.

Fugitive Releases : The venting emission was significantly reduced by installing control valves on the dehumidifier cooling water exit which allowed control of the water level in the bottom of the dehumidifier.  By controlling the water level, entrained reformed gas is allowed to escape (within the dehumidifier) prior to entering the hot well therefore significantly reducing Reformed Gas (which contains amounts of natural gas) from venting to the atmosphere.
